source.title: 2023 AHA/ACC/ACCP/ASPC/NLA/PCNA Guideline for the Management of Patients With Chronic Coronary Disease
source.organization: American Heart Association/American College of Cardiology

signals: Guideline for the Management of Patients With Chronic Coronary Disease
American Heart Association/American College of Cardiology

extraction.summary: The 2023 AHA/ACC/ACCP/ASPC/NLA/PCNA guidelines provide a comprehensive, evidence-based approach to managing patients with chronic coronary disease (CCD). The guidelines emphasize patient-centered, team-based care incorporating shared decision-making, social determinants of health, and cost-value considerations. Key updates include new recommendations for beta-blockers, SGLT2 inhibitors, GLP-1 receptor agonists, and shorter durations of dual antiplatelet therapy.

population.age_criteria: Not specifically defined; includes special considerations for young adults and older adults
population.risk_criteria: Patients with established chronic coronary disease including those with prior myocardial infarction, stable angina, and coronary revascularization
population.disease_definition: Chronic coronary disease (CCD) - previously termed stable ischemic heart disease
population.exclusions: Acute coronary syndromes in active phase
population.severity_staging: Risk stratification based on clinical assessment, functional status, and anatomic considerations

key_thresholds.context: Risk stratification for cardiovascular death or nonfatal MI
Risk stratification for cardiovascular death or nonfatal MI
Risk stratification for cardiovascular death or nonfatal MI
Global reduced levels indicating disease extent and severity
Minimum stent size for accurate patency assessment by coronary CTA
Maximum recommended, with optimal target of 1,500 mg/day

key_thresholds.parameter: LDL-C level for additional therapy consideration
key_thresholds.context: Threshold for adding ezetimibe or PCSK9 inhibitor
Exercise capacity assessment for sexual activity safety
Definition of one standard alcoholic drink
Target threshold for patients not at very high risk
Target BP for hypertensive CCD patients
Target weight reduction with lifestyle measures
Associated with >10% increase in odds of CCD progression and acute MI
Low-dose for secondary prevention (75-100mg range acceptable)
Standard duration followed by single antiplatelet therapy
May be considered in low bleeding risk, high thrombotic risk patients
Threshold for beta-blocker and ACE inhibitor recommendations
Threshold for prasugrel contraindication and edoxaban dosing
Minimum heart rate for ivabradine consideration
Threshold for moderate to severe LV dysfunction
Threshold for abnormal coronary microvascular resistance

recommendations.recommendation: Nonpharmacologic therapies, including healthy dietary habits and exercise, are recommended for all patients with chronic coronary disease

Long-term beta-blocker therapy is not recommended to improve outcomes in patients with CCD in the absence of myocardial infarction in the past year, left ventricular ejection fraction ≤50%, or another primary indication
Either a calcium channel blocker or beta blocker is recommended as first-line antianginal therapy
Use of sodium glucose cotransporter 2 inhibitors and glucagon-like peptide-1 receptor agonists are recommended for select groups of patients with CCD, including groups without diabetes

The use of nonprescription or dietary supplements, including fish oil and omega-3 fatty acids or vitamins, is not recommended in patients with CCD given the lack of benefit in reducing cardiovascular events
Routine periodic anatomic or ischemic testing without a change in clinical or functional status is not recommended for risk stratification or to guide therapeutic decision-making in patients with CCD

In patients with CCD and change in symptoms/functional capacity despite GDMT, stress PET/SPECT MPI, CMR imaging, or stress echocardiography is recommended to detect ischemia presence/extent, estimate MACE risk, and guide therapeutic decision-making
recommendations.notes: Modified from 2021 AHA/ACC chest pain guideline

recommendations.recommendation: PET is reasonable in preference to SPECT for nuclear MPI when available to improve diagnostic accuracy and decrease nondiagnostic test rates
recommendations.notes: In patients with persistent symptoms despite GDMT

recommendations.recommendation: Risk stratification incorporating all available information including noninvasive, invasive, or both cardiovascular diagnostic testing results or use validated risk scores
Optimization of GDMT to reduce MACE
ICA for patients with newly reduced LV systolic function, clinical heart failure, or both

ICA for risk stratification is not routinely recommended in patients without LV systolic dysfunction, heart failure, stable chest pain refractory to GDMT, and/or noninvasive testing suggestive of significant (>50%) left main disease
Clinical follow-up at least annually to assess for symptoms, change in functional status, adherence to and adequacy of lifestyle and medical interventions, and monitoring for complications
Multidisciplinary team-based approach to improve health outcomes, facilitate modification of ASCVD risk factors, and improve health service utilization
Patients with CCD should receive ongoing individualized education on symptom management, lifestyle changes, and SDOH risk factors
recommendations.notes: To improve knowledge and facilitate behavior change

recommendations.recommendation: Patients with CCD and their clinicians should engage in shared decision-making
recommendations.notes: Particularly when evidence is unclear or significant risk/benefit tradeoffs exist

recommendations.recommendation: Routine assessment by clinicians and care team for SDOH is recommended
recommendations.notes: To inform patient-centered treatment decisions and lifestyle change recommendations

recommendations.recommendation: Diet emphasizing vegetables, fruits, legumes, nuts, whole grains, and lean protein is recommended
recommendations.notes: To reduce risk of CVD events

recommendations.recommendation: Use of nonprescription dietary supplements is not beneficial
recommendations.notes: Including omega-3, vitamins C,D,E, beta-carotene, and calcium

recommendations.recommendation: Targeted discussions and screening for mental health conditions in patients with CCD
recommendations.notes: COR 2a - Should assess and refer for additional mental health evaluation

recommendations.recommendation: Treatment for mental health conditions with pharmacologic or non-pharmacologic therapies
Assess tobacco use at every healthcare visit
recommendations.notes: COR 1 - To facilitate identification of patients who may benefit from interventions

recommendations.recommendation: Advise patients who smoke tobacco to quit at every visit
Behavioral interventions combined with pharmacotherapy for tobacco cessation
recommendations.notes: COR 1 - Including bupropion, varenicline, or combination NRT

recommendations.recommendation: Routine screening and counseling about substance use
Limit alcohol intake if consuming alcohol
recommendations.notes: COR 2a - ≤1 drink/day for women, ≤2 drinks/day for men

recommendations.recommendation: Do not advise alcohol consumption for cardiovascular protection
recommendations.notes: COR 3 - Should not recommend alcohol for cardioprotection

recommendations.recommendation: High-intensity statin therapy to achieve ≥50% reduction in LDL-C levels
recommendations.notes: First-line therapy for all CCD patients unless contraindicated

recommendations.recommendation: Moderate-intensity statin therapy when high-intensity is contraindicated or not tolerated
Add ezetimibe for very high-risk patients with LDL-C ≥70 mg/dL on maximally tolerated statin
Screen for substance abuse using validated single-question screening tools
recommendations.notes: Can be self-administered in primary care settings

recommendations.recommendation: Individualize sexual activity resumption based on exercise capacity and type of activity
recommendations.notes: Sexual activity represents 3-5 METs of exercise

recommendations.recommendation: Maximally tolerated statin plus ezetimibe for LDL-C lowering
recommendations.notes: May be insufficient when ≥25% LDL-C reduction needed

recommendations.recommendation: Blood pressure target <130/80 mmHg in CCD patients with hypertension
recommendations.notes: Reduces CV events and all-cause death

recommendations.recommendation: First-line antihypertensive therapy with ACE inhibitors, ARBs, or beta-blockers
SGLT2 inhibitor or GLP-1 receptor agonist in CCD patients with type 2 diabetes
SGLT2 inhibitor in CCD patients with heart failure and LVEF ≤40%
Nonpharmacologic strategies as first-line therapy for elevated BP
Assessment of BMI with or without waist circumference during routine clinical follow-up
recommendations.notes: For weight management evaluation in CCD patients

recommendations.recommendation: Counseling on diet, lifestyle, and weight loss goals for overweight/obese CCD patients
recommendations.notes: Multicomponent interventions more effective than single components

recommendations.recommendation: GLP-1 receptor agonist for weight reduction when pharmacologic therapy warranted
recommendations.notes: Reasonable to choose semaglutide over liraglutide

recommendations.recommendation: Referral for bariatric procedure consideration in severe obesity
recommendations.notes: When lifestyle and pharmacologic interventions insufficient

recommendations.recommendation: All CCD patients with appropriate indications should be referred to cardiac rehabilitation
recommendations.notes: After MI, PCI, CABG, or with stable angina

recommendations.recommendation: Exercise regimen including ≥150 min/wk moderate-intensity or ≥75 min/wk high-intensity aerobic activities
recommendations.notes: Improves functional capacity, QOL, and reduces mortality

recommendations.recommendation: Resistance training exercises on ≥2 days/week
recommendations.notes: Improves muscle strength and functional capacity

recommendations.recommendation: Minimization of ambient air pollution exposure
Minimize exposure to ambient air pollution to reduce cardiovascular events
Low-dose aspirin 81mg (75-100mg) for patients with CCD and no indication for oral anticoagulation
recommendations.notes: Class 1 recommendation for atherosclerotic event reduction

recommendations.recommendation: DAPT with aspirin and clopidogrel for 6 months post-PCI followed by SAPT
recommendations.notes: Class 1 recommendation to reduce MACE and bleeding events

recommendations.recommendation: P2Y12 inhibitor monotherapy for at least 12 months after completing 1-3 months DAPT in select DES patients
recommendations.notes: Class 2a recommendation to reduce bleeding risk

recommendations.recommendation: Extended DAPT beyond 12 months for up to 3 years in previous MI patients at low bleeding risk
In patients with CCD and LVEF ≤40% with or without previous MI, the use of beta-blocker therapy is recommended to reduce the risk of future MACE, including cardiovascular death
In patients with CCD and LVEF <50%, the use of sustained release metoprolol succinate, carvedilol, or bisoprolol with titration to target doses is recommended in preference to other beta blockers
In patients with CCD who also have hypertension, diabetes, LVEF ≤40%, or CKD, the use of ACE inhibitors, or ARBs if ACE inhibitor–intolerant, is recommended to reduce cardiovascular events
In patients with CCD, the addition of colchicine for secondary prevention may be considered to reduce recurrent ASCVD events
In patients with CCD, an annual influenza vaccination is recommended to reduce cardiovascular morbidity, cardiovascular death, and all-cause death
Annual influenza vaccination for patients with CCD
recommendations.notes: Reduces cardiovascular morbidity, cardiovascular death, and all-cause death

recommendations.recommendation: COVID-19 vaccination per public health guidelines
Pneumococcal vaccine for patients with CCD
recommendations.notes: Reasonable to reduce cardiovascular morbidity and mortality

recommendations.recommendation: Antianginal therapy with beta blocker, CCB, or long-acting nitrate
Addition of second antianginal agent from different class if symptomatic
recommendations.notes: For patients remaining symptomatic after initial treatment

recommendations.recommendation: Ranolazine for patients symptomatic despite standard therapy
recommendations.notes: For those remaining symptomatic despite beta blockers, CCB, or long-acting nitrates

recommendations.recommendation: Ivabradine addition to standard anti-anginal therapy in patients with normal LV function is potentially harmful
recommendations.notes: Associated with higher risk of death or MI

recommendations.recommendation: CABG for significant left main disease or multivessel disease with severe LV dysfunction
recommendations.notes: Improves survival over medical therapy alone

recommendations.recommendation: In patients with CCD requiring revascularization for significant left main involvement associated with high-complexity CAD, CABG is recommended in preference to PCI to improve survival
In patients with CCD requiring revascularization for multivessel CAD with complex and diffuse CAD (eg, SYNTAX score >33), it is reasonable to choose CABG over PCI to improve survival

In patients with CCD, diabetes, and multivessel CAD with involvement of the left anterior descending artery who are appropriate candidates for CABG, CABG is recommended in preference to PCI to reduce mortality and repeat revascularizations
recommendations.notes: Class 1 recommendation with LIMA to LAD

recommendations.recommendation: In patients with CCD who have experienced SCAD, counseling should be provided regarding potential triggers and risk of SCAD recurrence
In symptomatic patients with nonobstructive CAD, a strategy of stratified medical therapy guided by invasive coronary physiologic testing can be useful for improving angina severity and QOL

medications.indication: Select CCD patients including those without diabetes
Select CCD patients including those without diabetes
Adjunctive lipid therapy in select populations
Referenced for lipid management in CCD
Depression in CCD patients with recent ACS
High-intensity statin therapy for LDL-C reduction
High-intensity statin therapy for LDL-C reduction
Very high-risk patients with LDL-C ≥70 mg/dL on statin plus ezetimibe
LDL-C lowering when other therapies insufficient
Secondary prevention in CCD without anticoagulation indication
DAPT post-PCI, monotherapy after DAPT completion
Previous MI without stroke/TIA/ICH history, added to aspirin
High ischemic risk, low-moderate bleeding risk, added to aspirin
Reduce GI bleeding risk in patients on DAPT

medications.dose: 5 mg twice daily or 2.5 mg twice daily
medications.indication: Anticoagulation in AF patients undergoing PCI

medications.dose: Target 25 mg twice daily (50 mg twice daily if >84kg)

red_flags: Change in clinical or functional status requiring reassessment
New onset or worsening angina symptoms
Change in symptoms or functional capacity that persists despite guideline-directed medical therapy
New-onset or worsening chest pain symptoms
Newly reduced left ventricular systolic function
New-onset or persistent stable chest pain
Frequent angina or severe stress-induced ischemia
Social determinants barriers affecting care access
Concomitant use of phosphodiesterase type 5 inhibitors with nitrate medications
Heavy alcohol use or binge drinking patterns
Unstable or decompensated CCD symptoms during sexual activity
Signs of statin-induced myopathy or rhabdomyolysis
Tendon rupture with bempedoic acid use
Incident atrial fibrillation with omega-3 fatty acids
Elevation in uric acid levels with bempedoic acid
History of stroke, TIA, or intracranial hemorrhage (contraindication for vorapaxar and prasugrel)
Significant increase in bleeding events on antiplatelet therapy
Exposure to extreme temperatures with medication-related risk (loop diuretics)
PM2.5 levels >10 mg/m³ with cardiovascular symptoms
History of TIA or stroke (contraindication for prasugrel)
Advanced renal disease (eGFR <30 mL/min/m2) when considering colchicine
Canadian Cardiovascular Society Class III/IV symptoms
Drug-drug interactions with multiple antianginal agents
Recurrent SCAD with extension of initial dissection
Procedural type 4a or type 5 MIs during revascularization
Signs of underlying vasculopathy or connective tissue disorders
Complex coronary anatomy requiring heart team evaluation
extraction.follow_up: Patient follow-up and monitoring should be individualized based on clinical status. Routine periodic anatomic or ischemic testing without clinical change is not recommended. Regular assessment of symptoms, functional capacity, medication adherence, and risk factor control.
extraction.escalation_path: Escalate care for changes in clinical or functional status, worsening symptoms, suspected acute events, medication intolerance or contraindications, or failure to achieve treatment goals. Consider cardiology consultation for complex cases or revascularization decisions.
